name: project-profile
purpose: Capture stack, commands, and project constraints to reduce blind decisions in later steps.
pitfall: Starting changes without documenting test commands or validating repository context.
whatItDoes: Centralizes baseline project context so every other skill starts from the same technical map.
whenToUse: At new repository kickoff, when inheriting code, or before a major iteration.
checklist: Identify safe zones and dangerous actions
suggestions: Keep the profile short but actionable
If stack or scripts change, update this skill first
Include commands actually used in CI/local
improvements: Include naming and branch policy conventions
Version key profile updates in changelog

projectPlayground.tweak: Split unit test command from contract test command
projectPlayground.expectedImpact: Less ambiguity when choosing validation depth
Better UX quality from early stages

name: plan-first
purpose: Define scope, risks, impacted files, and verification strategy before editing code.
pitfall: Jumping straight to implementation without measurable success criteria.
whatItDoes: Transforms a request into an executable plan with clear boundaries and validation.
whenToUse: Whenever the change touches multiple files or introduces new behavior.
suggestions: Use top-3 risks to prioritize tests
Tie each step to a verifiable artifact
improvements: Impact vs effort matrix for backlog

projectPlayground.tweak: Split plan into UX, data, validation, and tests

name: safe-changes
purpose: Encourage small diffs, checkpoints, and rollback paths for auditable reversible changes.
pitfall: Doing massive refactors without checkpoints or intermediate validation.
whatItDoes: Reduces implementation risk through incremental steps and frequent validation.
whenToUse: When critical flows may break or when multiple contributors work in parallel.
suggestions: Do not mix refactor + feature in one commit
If scope grows, pause and split into stages

projectPlayground.expectedImpact: Lower risk of broad UX breakage

name: code-review
purpose: Assess correctness, security, maintainability, and technical debt before closure.
pitfall: Reviewing only style while missing edge cases and contract impact.
whatItDoes: Applies structured review to find defects basic tests may miss.
whenToUse: Before merge/delivery, especially on logic or contract changes.

projectPlayground.tweak: Use a didactic checklist for junior devs

name: tests
purpose: Define test coverage for happy path, edge cases, and expected failure scenarios.
pitfall: Shipping behavior without regression tests or controlled-error assertions.
whatItDoes: Builds objective evidence that new and existing behavior remain healthy.
whenToUse: In any behavior change, bugfix, or contract evolution.
checklist: Add at least one key negative case
State what remains manual and why
suggestions: Avoid brittle tests tied to internal details
Isolate external dependencies to reduce flakes

projectPlayground.tweak: Model valid and invalid cases first
